preflight.mermaid_parses: not independently executed (no shell tool available to this critic invocation) - inferred valid because the provided diagram.png at artifacts/diagram.png was already successfully rendered from this exact .mmd source, which requires a clean mmdc parse
preflight.label_discipline: pass - all bold node labels (Task, __init__, first_task, second_task) are 1 word each, well under the 4-word cap; ghost nodes (SHARED, OVERRIDE) carry no <b> label, only annotation spans, consistent with the ghost-annotation pattern
preflight.concept_fidelity: pass - Task class/blueprint, __init__ constructor, class attribute category='general', instance attributes, first_task/second_task instances, and category override/shadowing all trace directly to corpus §5.2 (class vs instance, first_task/second_task naming, 'one class, many independent instances') and §5.3 (class attribute vs instance attribute, category shadowing mechanic). No unmatched/drifted labels.
preflight.title_discipline: pass - governing concept 'one class, many independent instances' (verbatim phrase from corpus §5.2) is correctly placed in the title: config field, not hung off as a dangling ghost node.
preflight.single_path_discipline: n/a - design intent is an explicit layered/branching flowchart (class -> constructor -> two sibling instances), not a linear pipeline, so the two constructor->instance edges are intentional, not a violation.

findings.detail: Clean TB layered tree: Task (blue) -> __init__ (purple) -> first_task / second_task (green), with two dotted ghost-annotation edges (class attr -> SHARED, locally shadows -> OVERRIDE). Matches design intent exactly: 6 nodes, 5 edges, no overlaps, no crossings, no truncation.
'instantiate', 'class attr', both 'Task(name, priority)' labels, and 'locally shadows' each sit in clear whitespace with no collision against nodes or other labels.

start (blue)=class blueprint, auto (purple)=constructor, done (green)=instances, ghost (transparent)=annotations - consistent, non-conflicting custom palette appropriate for an OOP diagram (not a HITL/auto pipeline, so the standard hitl/auto tier convention doesn't apply here).

findings.where: right side of canvas, level of TASKCLASS/CTOR rank
findings.detail: Minor, non-blocking: the SHARED ghost annotation to the right of __init__ leaves that right column visually sparser than the left column (which holds the full __init__ box). Column height/whitespace ratio is well under the 2x threshold that would fail this check, so no fix required, but if revising later, consider nudging rankSpacing down slightly or widening the SHARED annotation to balance density.

stats.png_dims: 1400px width (rendered at viewer scale, read via Read tool)
